description: Corporate headshot with eyeglasses. Face is clearly detected and the lighting is reasonably soft and frontal — but the eyeglass lens reflections occlude/distort the actual eye catchlights, making catchlight-position-based key direction unreliable. Per the catchlights-first doctrine, when catchlight reliability is degraded the engine should route INSUFFICIENT and surface the limitation rather than emit a confident pattern.

metadata.review_notes: Photographer-verified Phase 3A: face is detected and signal_strength is fine, so Phase 1 INSUFFICIENT gate does NOT fire. Phase 3A's catchlight_reliability axis covers no_face and earring_contamination but not eyeglasses. Phase 3B target — adding glasses-detection to catchlight_reliability will make this benchmark pass. The current engine routes this BOUNDED (via bootstrap path) at confidence 0.15 — extremely weak, an honest signal of measurement failure that the engine should escalate to INSUFFICIENT mode, not BOUNDED.
